title: Relationships Signals
summary: Relationships remain a live signal because technology, work, and changing norms keep reshaping how people meet, bond, and repair.

items.name: Loneliness is being treated as a social risk
items.summary: Governments and researchers are paying more attention to social isolation.
items.whyItMatters: Connection is being reframed as a wellbeing issue, not just a private concern.

items.name: Dating apps keep reshaping courtship
items.summary: Algorithms increasingly mediate first contact and partner selection.
items.whyItMatters: People now need to understand the incentives built into platforms.

items.name: Dating platforms
items.summary: App design influences who meets whom and how quickly.
items.whyItMatters: Product choices can alter the entire social experience of dating.

items.name: Therapy platforms
items.summary: Online counselling and coaching tools are growing quickly.
items.whyItMatters: Access to relationship support is becoming more digital.

items.name: Family courts
items.summary: Relationship conflict becomes formal and legal when families split.
items.whyItMatters: The legal system shapes the practical consequences of relational breakdown.

items.name: Psychology departments
items.summary: Research continues to refine what healthy relationships look like.
items.whyItMatters: The science side of relationships is becoming more precise.

items.name: John Gottman
items.summary: Research on conflict and repair keeps his work central.
items.whyItMatters: His work remains a practical baseline for modern relationship thinking.

items.name: Esther Perel
items.summary: A public voice on intimacy and desire.
items.whyItMatters: She helps translate relationship complexity into accessible language.

items.name: Are modern relationships too individualistic?
items.summary: Some argue the culture of self-optimisation weakens commitment.
items.whyItMatters: This debate shapes how people think about marriage, friendship, and family.

items.name: Can apps make intimacy easier?
items.summary: Technology can expand choice but also intensify comparison and disposability.
items.whyItMatters: This affects how people date and what they expect from connection.
